title: fraxinus ferruginea
intro: Fraxinus ferruginea, also known as the rusty buckeye, is a type of deciduous tree native to the United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bark and leaves of the rusty buckeye are said to have health benefits, such as reducing fever and relieving pain, and have been used to treat various ailments, including rheumatism and digestive issues. Fraxinus ferruginea can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in folk medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
